tweets.text:
Facts vs Feelings 🎙️ LIVE from Carson's Q2 Summit in Chicago! This week, @RyanDetrick & @sonusvarghese sit down with Nobel Prize winner Richard Thaler to discuss:

🏈 Why trading down wins the NFL draft (Tom Brady went 199)
🎢 Why markets swing way more than they should
📊 What it means when 10-sigma events keep showing up every decade
🚀 How the SpaceX IPO is about to test indexing at a scale we've never seen
📉 What buying IPOs on day one has cost investors (~30% over 3 years)
✅ The pre-checked box that did more for retirement saving than any class ever could
🏡 Why a $2M home equity gain barely moves spending and more!

All links below 👇 🎙️ 🔥

tweets.text:
Bearish investor positioning in gold options is extremely crowded:

The 6-month put-call skew on the largest US gold-backed ETF, $GLD, is up to 1.03, near the highest since 2017.

The put-call skew measures the relative cost of put options versus call options, rising when investors are paying more to protect against downside than to bet on further upside.

This metric has risen +0.13 points over the last few weeks, posting the largest increase since the 2022 bear market.

By comparison, the 10-year average of the 6-month put-call skew is 0.90.

Meanwhile, the 3-month put skew on $GLD is up to 1.19, the highest on record.

This measures demand for downside protection, and it rises when investors are willing to pay more to hedge against losses.

The short gold trade has become very crowded.
tweets.full_text:

The short gold trade has become very crowded.

BREAKING: The US and Iran are discussing moving up the signing of the Iran deal from Friday to potentially as soon as today, per Axios.

If that happens, the MOU would be signed electronically, the parts of the deal concerning the Strait of Hormuz would go into effect, and the full text may be released.
tweets.full_text:
BREAKING: The US and Iran are discussing moving up the signing of the Iran deal from Friday to potentially as soon as today, per Axios.

If that happens, the MOU would be signed electronically, the parts of the deal concerning the Strait of Hormuz would go into effect, and the full text may be released.

tweets.text:
"AI will transform pharma, but not evenly and not all at once."

"Building an enduring company in the age of AI is hard precisely because advantages can dissipate rapidly as models improve."

"The companies that win will predict the uneven frontiers correctly, absorbing each model improvement as a tailwind rather than a headwind."

"Our view: discovered drugs are becoming abundant while clinical development remains the binding constraint — so the durable position is built around that bottleneck, not in the path of advancing discovery models."

Formation Bio CEO Benjamine Liu on how AI will transform biopharma, and why the sequence of change matters: https://t.co/2fQ0M0Dg9G
tweets.full_text:
"AI will transform pharma, but not evenly and not all at once."

"Our view: discovered drugs are becoming abundant while clinical development remains the binding constraint — so the durable position is built around that bottleneck, not in the path of advancing discovery models."

tweets.text:
Where is the US' gold stockpile?

Fort Knox currently holds ~147.3 million troy ounces of gold, the most of any US location, representing ~60% of the total US gold reserves managed by the Treasury.

Fort Knox is the US Bullion Depository in Kentucky and has served as the primary storage site for gold owned outright by the US government since 1937.

By comparison, West Point in New York holds ~54.1 million troy ounces of gold, and Denver in Colorado holds ~43.9 million troy ounces.

Both facilities also serve as active US Mint coin production sites alongside their role as gold depositories.

Meanwhile, the New York Fed vault holds just ~13.4 million troy ounces, or 9% of Fort Knox.

Fort Knox is the backbone of US gold reserves.
tweets.full_text:
Where is the US' gold stockpile?

Fort Knox currently holds ~147.3 million troy ounces of gold, the most of any US location, representing ~60% of the total US gold reserves managed by the Treasury.

Fort Knox is the US Bullion Depository in Kentucky and has served as the primary storage site for gold owned outright by the US government since 1937.

By comparison, West Point in New York holds ~54.1 million troy ounces of gold, and Denver in Colorado holds ~43.9 million troy ounces.

Both facilities also serve as active US Mint coin production sites alongside their role as gold depositories.

Meanwhile, the New York Fed vault holds just ~13.4 million troy ounces, or 9% of Fort Knox.

Fort Knox is the backbone of US gold reserves.

We’re excited to lead Convey’s $38M Series A.

@ConveyAI lets non-technical teams build and run AI teammates that take entire categories of operational work off their plates - no engineering required.

You onboard Convey like a new hire: share your screen, walk through a process, and its agents own the outcome, running inside your existing systems and asking the right person when it gets stuck.

Convey teammates have already completed 1.1M+ hours of real work inside companies like NBCUniversal, TelevisaUnivision, Unity, Samsara, ChargePoint, and Faire.

Welcome, @rohanbchopra and the Convey team.

By @joeschmidtiv and @omooretweets

tweets.text:
Institutional investors are extremely bullish on US stocks:

Last week, hedge funds recorded their largest weekly purchases of US equities since November 2025.

This was primarily led by short covering rather than outright long purchases.

This also marks the 4th consecutive weekly purchase.

As a result, the information technology sector now accounts for ~22% of total US hedge fund equity exposure, near the highest in 5 years.

This percentage has surged +6 points since January, significantly outpacing the increase following the 2022 bear market recovery.

Hedge funds are benefiting from the tech rally.

tweets.text:
Non-farm payrolls came in at 172,000. Expectations were 88,000. Wages grew ~0.3%. Productivity is running near 3%. That gets you to unit labor costs of roughly half a percent. @CathieDWood explains why that is a boom story in “In The Know.”
https://t.co/36f5gsZeU3

tweets.text:
$XLU and $XLP are breaking down against $SPY, which means money is rotating out of defense because growth and cyclicals like $XLK and $XLI are still leading the tape. 

But the $VUG vs $VTV ratio just rolled off its highs, so the question is whether tech and industrials can take the baton if growth keeps slipping. 

If they can't, there's no leadership left on offense, and that's when money floods back into the utilities and staples.
tweets.full_text:

If they can't, there's no leadership left on offense, and that's when money floods back into the utilities and staples.

tweets.text:
The greenback just ran 4% off its January low back to ~99.5, while $BTC dropped ~46% from $120K to $65K

A stronger dollar $DXY drains the global liquidity that bids long-duration risk, and nothing is longer-duration than Bitcoin.  

Get the direction of the dollar right, and you get the direction of almost everything else.

thread_tweets.text:
No hike, no cut. 

That part is settled. 

The whole meeting lives in 3 places. 

The forward guidance language, the updated dot plot, and what Warsh says at the podium. 

Get those right and you know how the next leg trades.
thread_tweets.full_text:
No hike, no cut. 

That part is settled. 

The whole meeting lives in 3 places. 

The forward guidance language, the updated dot plot, and what Warsh says at the podium.

thread_tweets.text:
Second, the dots. 

March showed one cut for 2026. 

Inflation risk is higher now, so expect that to come out. 

Base case is a median of zero cuts for 2026, and 2027 could lose its cut too. 

A dot plot that prices nothing is the setup going in.

thread_tweets.text:
First, the guidance. 

One sentence has carried the easing bias for almost a year, the line about the timing of future adjustments to the target range. 

That sentence gets removed or rewritten today. 

The day it disappears, the Fed stops promising the next move is lower.

thread_tweets.text:
Base case: no rate change, easing bias removed or revised, median dot shows no cut. 

This is what the market already expects. 

It does not make a hike more likely, and with the war ending and energy set to fall, investors keep hoping for a late-2026 cut anyway.
thread_tweets.full_text:

thread_tweets.text:
Hawkish if: guidance turns to a hawkish tone or the dots imply a real chance of a hike. 

The median dot showing a hike is unlikely, but if a cluster of individual dots pencil in a 2026 hike, or the statement leans hard on inflation risk, that surprises this market.

thread_tweets.text:
Bigger question is independence. 

The fear is Warsh tilts toward the administration over the data. 

If the dots show no 2026 cut but he says he still wants one, the market sees a chair who isn’t his own man. 

Yields rise, stocks follow. 

The bullish move is proving he won’t.

tweets.full_text:
The Fed has been an afterthought since the Iran war started. 

That ends today. 

The rate decision is set in stone. 

The forward guidance, the dots, and Warsh’s first press conference are not. 

This is the meeting that can break the rally or clear the path to new highs 🧵 https://t.co/AFwDJwL4Cz

No hike, no cut. 

That part is settled. 

The whole meeting lives in 3 places. 

The forward guidance language, the updated dot plot, and what Warsh says at the podium. 

Get those right and you know how the next leg trades.

Second, the dots. 

March showed one cut for 2026. 

Inflation risk is higher now, so expect that to come out. 

Base case is a median of zero cuts for 2026, and 2027 could lose its cut too. 

A dot plot that prices nothing is the setup going in.

First, the guidance. 

One sentence has carried the easing bias for almost a year, the line about the timing of future adjustments to the target range. 

That sentence gets removed or rewritten today. 

The day it disappears, the Fed stops promising the next move is lower.

Base case: no rate change, easing bias removed or revised, median dot shows no cut. 

This is what the market already expects. 

It does not make a hike more likely, and with the war ending and energy set to fall, investors keep hoping for a late-2026 cut anyway.

Hawkish if: guidance turns to a hawkish tone or the dots imply a real chance of a hike. 

The median dot showing a hike is unlikely, but if a cluster of individual dots pencil in a 2026 hike, or the statement leans hard on inflation risk, that surprises this market.

Base Case Likely Market Reaction: 

$SPX little changed. 

Tech and AI lead. 

10yr drifts modestly lower on sell-the-news. 

$DXY drops. 

Both already rallied into this. 

Gold gets a mild bounce. 

Nothing here boosts hike fears, so the reaction stays muted.

Dovish if: guidance isn’t revised and/or the 2026 dots still show a cut. 

This is the Fed pushing back on hike fears and clearing the path higher. 

Least likely outcome given the inflation backdrop, but it’s the cleanest fuel for the next leg of the rally.

Hawkish Likely Market Reaction: 

$SPX down more than 1%. 

$XLK $VUG lead it lower, defensives outperform but still red. 

10yr jumps 10+ bps, likely back above 4.60%. 

$DXY rips near 1% above 100. 

Gold hit hard, down 1%+. 

A hawkish surprise can trigger a 3-5% pullback.

Dovish Likely Market Reaction: 

$SPX jumps close to 1% on a knee-jerk rally. 

Tech, growth and cyclicals lead, defensives lag but green. 

10yr down less than 10 bps, the wild card if bonds smell the Fed running it hot. 

$DXY falls sharply. 

Gold the biggest winner, up 1%+.

Bigger question is independence. 

The fear is Warsh tilts toward the administration over the data. 

If the dots show no 2026 cut but he says he still wants one, the market sees a chair who isn’t his own man. 

Yields rise, stocks follow. 

The bullish move is proving he won’t.

The real wildcard is Warsh. 

First meeting as chair. 

New chairs talk loosely early. 

Bernanke, Yellen and Powell all moved markets with an offhand line at the start. 

Do not be shocked if the press conference brings more volatility than the statement does.

If you’re already factoring monetary policy into your macro overlay, you’re ahead of most investors. 

Join 4,000 advisors and investors by subscribing to my weekly report 👇

https://t.co/ZkGXJSv6aS

@Lila79703094 👊

tweets.text:
DECISION IMMINENT: US energy regulator FERC is poised to rule by end of June on how data centers connect to the grid. Former FERC Chairman Neil Chatterjee calls it "the most significant FERC action in decades."

Two tracks to watch:
1) PJM co-location rules: clarifying whether hyperscalers can buy power directly from on-site generators without triggering tariff violations
2) A broader DOE-directed federal rulemaking that would standardize large load interconnection nationwide and may require hyperscalers to foot the full bill for grid upgrades

Existing grid interconnects become more valuable if hyperscalers must self-fund upgrades, but even more importantly, regulatory certainty alone is likely enough to kick off a new investment cycle in PJM, where new capacity has badly lagged demand. For context, PJM fell 6.6 GW short in its December 2025 capacity auction, with capacity prices hitting a record $333/MW-day.

Could be good catalyst for PJM-exposed names: $TLN $CEG $WULF $KEEL $MARA

I'm seeing a lot of panic around the pullback of GPU rental rates (specifically H100s).

Reminder that rental rates for old GPUs should decline over time.

In general, the rental decline rates have been much shallower than assumed when modeling DC IRRs. https://t.co/Lj0DMFtK50
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
Appreciate the flag, Shanu. 
To address your feedback: the EV/energized power multiples are intentionally a current-state comp, a mark-to-market look at what's in the ground today, not a projection. 
The capital structure work lives in the site-level SOTP and the funding gap analysis by name, which walks through the incremental debt and equity each company needs to deliver its pipeline.

tweets.text: Hello from Germany, where the auto crisis just deepened. BMW has issued a major profit warning, implying a profit drop of >60%. Its EBIT margin is now expected at just 1–3%; a shocking level for a premium automaker that once stood for double-digit profitability. This comes only a day after VW executives reportedly described their own company as facing an existential threat, according to Manager Magazin. BMW is now worth less <€40bn.

tweets.text: A lot of investors take Jevons’ paradox for granted. But up until recently, IT hardware spending was flat-to-down as a share of GDP, even as Moore’s law continued to hold. Why? Because prices usually fell more quickly than the volume of sales rose. https://t.co/AyO45vMM67
